title: cassipourea korupensis
intro: Cassipourea korupensis, also known as the korup cherry, is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Rhizophoraceae. It has been traditionally used in West African medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Cassipourea korupensis include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antihelmintic. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are used to treat a range of ailments.
